get.description: Given a list of fields, checks for their existence in the business vault without decrypting them.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma separated list of fields to check. For example, `id.first_name,id.ssn4,custom.bank_account`

delete.description: Deletes data in a business vault.

patch.description: Updates data in a business vault.

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields from the provided vault.
Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault.
Updates data in a business vault.

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: Specify the content type of the object like `application/json` or `image/png`

post.description: Generates a single-use session token for an onboarding configuration.
Validate a short-lived onboarding session token and exchange it for a long-lived fp_id
Creates a new user vault, optionally initializing with the provided data

parameters.name: x-idempotency-id
parameters.description: To safely support retrying requests without accidentally performing the same operation multiple times, provide a client-generated `x-idempotency-id`. Requests made with the same `x-idempotency-id` value will no-op and return the same result. Note, if `x-idempotency-id` is provided, initial data may not be specified in the HTTP body.

patch.description: Updates data in a vault given a short-lived, entity-scoped client token.

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields given a short-lived, entity-scoped client token

get.description: Decrypts and downloads the user's data as specified by the provided token.

post.description: Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault given a short-lived, user-scoped client token
Updates data in a vault given a short-lived, entity-scoped client token.

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: Specify the content type of the object like `application/json` or `image/png`

get.description: View details of a most recent onboarding status of user

post.description: Create a short-lived token safe to pass to your client for operations to vault or decrypt data for this user.

get.description: Given a list of fields, checks for their existence in the user vault without decrypting them.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma separated list of fields to check. For example, `id.first_name,id.ssn4,custom.bank_account`

delete.description: Deletes data in a user vault.

patch.description: Updates data in a user vault.

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields from the provided vault.
Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault.
Updates data in a user vault.

parameters.name: content-type
parameters.description: Specify the content type of the object like `application/json` or `image/png`

post.description: Invoke the vault proxy 'just-in-time' (JIT) to securely send and receive data to a target destination

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-target-url
parameters.description: Target proxy destination URL Required if "just-in-time" OR ignored if using proxy configuration

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-method
parameters.description: HTTP Method VERB for the proxy destination request (defaults to POST)

parameters.name: x-fp-path-and-query
parameters.description: Egress destination URL path and query string to append

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-content-type
parameters.description: Content-type for the proxy ingress

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-access-reason
parameters.description: Access reason for any egress decryption operations during the proxy request

parameters.name: x-fp-id
parameters.description: When proxy requests are on behalf of a single footprint vault, you can can omit the `fp_id_` prefix on token identifiers, and just use `id.x` or `custom.y` instead of `fp_id_xyz.id.x` or `fp_id_xyz.custom.y`.  Similarly, if specifying proxy configuration ingress rules from a stored configuration the corresponding token must be assigned just-in-time via a headers  i.e: `x-fp-id: fp_id_abc`

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule
parameters.description: Configure one more ingress rules  x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_number=$.data.card.number x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_exp=$.data.card.expiration x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_cvc=$.data.card.security_code

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-fwd-
parameters.description: Prefixes custom headers to forward along egress For example `x-fp-proxy-fwd-MYHEADER: hello world` sends `MYHEADER: hello world` to the destination

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-cert
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client certificate to use (required if using key)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-key
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client key to use (required if using cert)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-pin-cert
parameters.description: Configure one or more base64 encoded PEM server certificates to validate and pin proxy destination TLS connections.

post.description: Invoke the vault proxy by configuration id to securely send and receive data to a target destination

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-method
parameters.description: HTTP Method VERB for the proxy destination request (defaults to POST)

parameters.name: x-fp-path-and-query
parameters.description: Egress destination URL path and query string to append

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-content-type
parameters.description: Content-type for the proxy ingress

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-access-reason
parameters.description: Access reason for any egress decryption operations during the proxy request

parameters.name: x-fp-id
parameters.description: When proxy requests are on behalf of a single footprint vault, you can can omit the `fp_id_` prefix on token identifiers, and just use `id.x` or `custom.y` instead of `fp_id_xyz.id.x` or `fp_id_xyz.custom.y`.  Similarly, if specifying proxy configuration ingress rules from a stored configuration the corresponding token must be assigned just-in-time via a headers  i.e: `x-fp-id: fp_id_abc`

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule
parameters.description: Configure one more ingress rules  x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_number=$.data.card.number x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_exp=$.data.card.expiration x-fp-proxy-ingress-rule: fp_id_abc.custom.credit_card_cvc=$.data.card.security_code

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-fwd-
parameters.description: Prefixes custom headers to forward along egress For example `x-fp-proxy-fwd-MYHEADER: hello world` sends `MYHEADER: hello world` to the destination

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-cert
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client certificate to use (required if using key)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-client-key
parameters.description: Base64 encoded PEM client key to use (required if using cert)

parameters.name: x-fp-proxy-pin-cert
parameters.description: Configure one or more base64 encoded PEM server certificates to validate and pin proxy destination TLS connections.

Secret API Key.name: X-Footprint-Secret-Key
Secret API Key.description: Secret API key. You can create and view your API keys in the dashboard. This key should never be sent to your client and should be treated as a secret on your server.

Dashboard Token.name: X-Fp-Dashboard-Authorization
Dashboard Token.description: Short-lived token for an authenticated dashboard user.

Client Token.name: X-Fp-Authorization
Client Token.description: Short-lived client token to perform actions for a given user.

ValidateRequest.description:
Request containing a short-lived validation token that is used to verify auth and the end of
 an onboarding session.

validation_token.description: An cryptographically generated auth token to authenticate a session

fields.description: List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`, `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

filters.description:
A list of filter functions to apply to each decrypted data
 Omit or leave empty to apply no filters

items.description: Represents a data transform to apply to underlying plaintext behind a data identifier. Specify only one of the following fields. See more here: https://docs.onefootprint.com/vault/proxy#filter-functions

public_key.description: hex encoded, DER-formatted asymmetric public key

reason.description: Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged

validation_token.description: An cryptographically generated auth token to authenticate a session

expires_at.description: The time at which this token will expire.

vault_fields.description: The list of fields that are allowed to be vaulted by this token

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

decrypt_reason.description: If the token is allowed to decrypt, provide a default decryption reason

fields.description: List of data identifiers to which this token will have access. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`, `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

scopes.description: Specify whether this token should be allowed to vault, decrypt, or both

ttl.description: Time to live until this token expires, provided in seconds. Defaults to 30 minutes. Must be at least 60 seconds, at most 1 day

session_token.description: a one-time use session token for onboarding a new user

fields.description: List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`, `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

filters.description:
A list of filter functions to apply to each decrypted data
 Omit or leave empty to apply no filters

items.description: Represents a data transform to apply to underlying plaintext behind a data identifier. Specify only one of the following fields. See more here: https://docs.onefootprint.com/vault/proxy#filter-functions

public_key.description: hex encoded, DER-formatted asymmetric public key

reason.description:
Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged.
 The reason must be provided either here or in the client token

fields.description: List of data identifiers to delete. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`, `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.
